name: Pizza Bombs
instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 190 degrees Celsius. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper for easy cleanup.

2. Open the refrigerated biscuit can and separate into individual biscuits. Using your thumb or the palm of your hand, gently flatten each biscuit into a thin circle, working from the center outward to create an even thickness.

3. In a small bowl, mix the tomato sauce with dried oregano until well combined. Spoon the seasoned sauce onto the center of each flattened biscuit circle, spreading it evenly but leaving a small border around the edge for sealing.

4. Shred the mozzarella cheese if not pre-shredded. Sprinkle the cheese over the sauce on each circle, then add diced pepperoni pieces on top.

5. Working with one circle at a time, carefully pull up the edges of the dough from all sides to meet in the center, forming a ball shape. Pinch the seams together firmly to seal completely, ensuring no filling can escape. Gently roll each ball between your palms to smooth the surface and reinforce the seal.

6. Place each ball seam-side down on the prepared baking sheet, spacing them evenly. Melt the butter in the microwave or on the stovetop, then use a pastry brush to coat each ball generously with melted butter.

7. Grate the Parmesan cheese and sprinkle it over the tops of the balls. Finish with a light dusting of Italian seasoning for extra flavor.

8. Bake in the preheated oven until the tops are golden brown and the dough is cooked through, about 15 minutes. The balls should sound hollow when tapped. Serve hot with additional warmed tomato sauce on the side for dipping.
simpleInstructions:
1. Preheat oven and line baking sheet.

2. Flatten biscuits into circles.

3. Add seasoned sauce, cheese, and pepperoni to each.

4. Form into sealed balls.

5. Place on sheet and brush with melted butter.

6. Top with Parmesan and Italian seasoning.

7. Bake until golden and serve with sauce.
